description: Add comprehensive plugin lifecycle hooks to the plugin SDK and adapter runtime. Plugins can optionally implement onNavigate (URL changes), onActivate/onDeactivate (injection lifecycle), and onToolInvocationStart/onToolInvocationEnd (tool execution observability). The adapter IIFE build wires all hooks automatically. Full E2E test coverage via the e2e-test plugin.

userStories.title: Add onNavigate hook to plugin SDK and adapter runtime
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want an onNavigate hook so my plugin can react to in-page URL changes (SPA navigations, hash changes) without requiring a full page reload.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenTabsPlugin base class in platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts has optional onNavigate?(url: string): void
The hook is called when the page URL changes via pushState, replaceState, popstate, or hashchange
The hook receives the new URL as a string argument
If the plugin does not implement onNavigate, no listeners are set up (no overhead)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts — add `onNavigate?(url: string): void` to the OpenTabsPlugin abstract class, alongside the existing teardown?() method. Add a JSDoc comment explaining that this hook fires on in-page URL changes (pushState, replaceState, popstate, hashchange) and runs in the page context. The hook signature takes the new URL as a string. This story only adds the SDK interface — the wiring happens in US-004. Publish will happen in US-006.

userStories.title: Add onActivate and onDeactivate hooks
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want onActivate/onDeactivate hooks so my plugin can set up and tear down resources tied to the adapter's injection lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenTabsPlugin has optional onActivate?(): void
OpenTabsPlugin has optional onDeactivate?(): void
onActivate is called once when the adapter is first injected and registered on globalThis.__openTabs.adapters
onDeactivate is called when the adapter is being removed (before teardown, or when navigating away)
onDeactivate fires before teardown() if both are defined
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts — add `onActivate?(): void` and `onDeactivate?(): void` to OpenTabsPlugin. onActivate JSDoc: 'Called once after the adapter is registered on globalThis.__openTabs.adapters. Use for setting up page-level event listeners, observers, or other resources that should exist for the adapter's lifetime.' onDeactivate JSDoc: 'Called when the adapter is being removed — either before teardown() on plugin update, or when the tab navigates away from a matching URL. Use for cleanup of resources set up in onActivate.' Note the relationship: onDeactivate is called BEFORE teardown. If a plugin implements both onDeactivate and teardown, onDeactivate fires first for lifecycle-aware cleanup, then teardown fires for the existing cleanup path. This story only adds the SDK interface — wiring happens in US-004.

userStories.title: Add onToolInvocationStart and onToolInvocationEnd hooks
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want hooks around tool invocations so I can implement logging, metrics, or UI feedback when tools are called.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenTabsPlugin has optional onToolInvocationStart?(toolName: string): void
OpenTabsPlugin has optional onToolInvocationEnd?(toolName: string, success: boolean, durationMs: number): void
onToolInvocationStart is called before tool.handle() executes
onToolInvocationEnd is called after tool.handle() completes (success or failure)
durationMs measures the wall-clock time of tool.handle() in milliseconds
success is true if handle() resolved, false if it threw
If onToolInvocationStart or onToolInvocationEnd throw, the error is logged but does not affect tool execution
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts — add `onToolInvocationStart?(toolName: string): void` and `onToolInvocationEnd?(toolName: string, success: boolean, durationMs: number): void` to OpenTabsPlugin. JSDoc for onToolInvocationStart: 'Called before each tool.handle() execution. Runs in the page context. Receives the tool name (unprefixed, e.g. "send_message" not "slack_send_message"). Errors thrown here are caught and logged — they do not prevent tool execution.' JSDoc for onToolInvocationEnd: 'Called after each tool.handle() completes, whether it succeeded or threw. Receives the tool name, a boolean indicating success, and the duration in milliseconds. Errors thrown here are caught and logged.' This story only adds the SDK interface — the wiring (wrapping tool.handle in the IIFE) happens in US-004.

userStories.title: Update adapter IIFE build to wire lifecycle hooks
userStories.description: As the platform, I want the opentabs-plugin build command to generate IIFE code that automatically wires all lifecycle hooks so plugin developers get them for free.
acceptanceCriteria: The generated IIFE wrapper in bundleIIFE (platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts) sets up all lifecycle hooks
onActivate: called after the adapter is registered on globalThis.__openTabs.adapters[name]
onDeactivate: called during teardown (before plugin.teardown() if both exist)
onNavigate: intercepts history.pushState, history.replaceState, and listens for popstate + hashchange events; calls plugin.onNavigate(location.href) on URL changes
onToolInvocationStart/End: wraps each tool.handle() to call the hooks before and after execution with timing
All hook calls are wrapped in try-catch to prevent hook errors from breaking tool execution or adapter registration
Cleanup: the teardown function removes URL change listeners and restores original history methods
The onNavigate listener setup only happens if the plugin implements onNavigate (checked at runtime)
The tool wrapping only happens if the plugin implements onToolInvocationStart or onToolInvocationEnd
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for all lifecycle hooks
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests verifying all lifecycle hooks fire correctly with the expected arguments so I can prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e-test plugin implements all lifecycle hooks (onActivate, onDeactivate, onNavigate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd)
Each hook sets markers on globalThis for E2E verification (e.g., __opentabs_onActivate_called, __opentabs_onNavigate_urls, etc.)
E2E test: onActivate fires when adapter is injected — verify __opentabs_onActivate_called is true after navigating to a matching tab

E2E test: onDeactivate fires on adapter removal — verify __opentabs_onDeactivate_called is true after plugin.update triggers re-injection (the old adapter's onDeactivate fires before the new one's onActivate)
E2E test: onNavigate fires on pushState — call history.pushState in the page, verify __opentabs_onNavigate_urls contains the new URL
E2E test: onToolInvocationStart fires before tool execution — call e2e-test_echo tool, verify __opentabs_tool_invocation_start contains 'echo'
E2E test: onToolInvocationEnd fires after tool execution with correct args — verify __opentabs_tool_invocation_end contains { toolName: 'echo', success: true, durationMs: <number> }
E2E test: onToolInvocationEnd reports failure for a failing tool — call e2e-test_failing_tool, verify success is false
bun run test:e2e passes

userStories.title: Publish packages and update plugins
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want to publish the updated SDK and plugin-tools packages so that the e2e-test plugin can depend on the new lifecycle hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Bump version in platform/shared, platform/plugin-sdk, platform/plugin-tools
Publish packages in dependency order: shared → plugin-sdk → plugin-tools
Wait for npm propagation
Update plugins/e2e-test/package.json to use the new SDK version
cd plugins/e2e-test && bun install && bun run build succeeds
All platform quality gates pass: bun run build, type-check, lint, knip, test
userStories.notes: Use the existing publish script: ./scripts/publish.sh <new-version>. The script publishes shared, plugin-sdk, and plugin-tools in dependency order. After publishing, wait ~30s for npm CDN propagation. Then update plugins/e2e-test/package.json dependencies: @opentabs-dev/shared and @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk to the new version. Run bun install in plugins/e2e-test to fetch the updated packages. Run bun run build (which runs tsc && opentabs-plugin build) to verify the e2e-test plugin compiles and bundles correctly with the new lifecycle hooks. Also update plugins/slack if it exists, same process. Run all platform quality gates to verify nothing broke.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md with lifecycle hooks documentation
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want CLAUDE.md to document the new lifecycle hooks so that anyone working on the codebase understands the available plugin hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md Key Concepts section mentions lifecycle hooks
Lists available hooks: onNavigate, onActivate, onDeactivate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd
Explains that hooks are optional and wired automatically by the IIFE build
Does not enumerate individual plugins — keeps the documentation plugin-agnostic
All quality gates pass
userStories.notes:
Edit CLAUDE.md — add a 'Lifecycle hooks' subsection under Key Concepts (after the 'Tab state machine' section). Keep it concise:

**Lifecycle hooks**: Plugins can optionally implement lifecycle hooks on the `OpenTabsPlugin` base class. All hooks are wired automatically by the `opentabs-plugin build` command in the generated IIFE wrapper — plugin authors only need to implement the methods.

- `onActivate()` — called once after the adapter is registered on `globalThis.__openTabs.adapters`
- `onDeactivate()` — called when the adapter is being removed (before `teardown()`)
- `onNavigate(url)` — called on in-page URL changes (pushState, replaceState, popstate, hashchange)
- `onToolInvocationStart(toolName)` — called before each `tool.handle()` execution
- `onToolInvocationEnd(toolName, success, durationMs)` — called after each `tool.handle()` completes

All hooks run in the page context. Errors in hooks are caught and logged — they do not affect adapter registration or tool execution.
